entry.title: A Regret Minimization Framework on Preference Learning in Large Language Models

link.title: A Regret Minimization Framework on Preference Learning in Large Language Models

entry.content: RePO (Regret-based Preference Optimization) reframes RLHF as regret minimization rather than reward maximization. Motivated by the prospective and counterfactual nature of human feedback, RePO models preferences as behavior-conditioned assessments of relative suboptimality. It admits a closed-form update under KL-regularized RL and integrates seamlessly with direct preference optimization. Empirically, RePO achieves consistent gains on mathematical reasoning and human preference benchmarks, demonstrating a principled and effective alternative to reward-based RLHF.
entry.summary: RePO (Regret-based Preference Optimization) reframes RLHF as regret minimization rather than reward maximization. Motivated by the prospective and counterfactual nature of human feedback, RePO models preferences as behavior-conditioned assessments of relative suboptimality. It admits a closed-form update under KL-regularized RL and integrates seamlessly with direct preference optimization. Empirically, RePO achieves consistent gains on mathematical reasoning and human preference benchmarks, demonstrating a principled and effective alternative to reward-based RLHF.

entry.title: MVP-LAM: Learning Action-Centric Latent Action via Cross-Viewpoint Reconstruction

link.title: MVP-LAM: Learning Action-Centric Latent Action via Cross-Viewpoint Reconstruction

entry.content: Multi-View Point Latent Action Model (MVP-LAM) learns discrete latent actions that are highly informative about ground-truth actions from time-synchronized multi-view videos. By training with a cross-viewpoint reconstruction objective, where a latent action inferred from one view must explain the future in another view, MVP-LAM reduces reliance on viewpoint-specific cues and produces more action-centric representations. On Bridge V2 it achieves higher mutual information with ground-truth actions and improved action prediction, and pretraining VLAs with MVP-LAM latent actions improves downstream manipulation performance on the SIMPLER and LIBERO-Long benchmarks.
entry.summary: Multi-View Point Latent Action Model (MVP-LAM) learns discrete latent actions that are highly informative about ground-truth actions from time-synchronized multi-view videos. By training with a cross-viewpoint reconstruction objective, where a latent action inferred from one view must explain the future in another view, MVP-LAM reduces reliance on viewpoint-specific cues and produces more action-centric representations. On Bridge V2 it achieves higher mutual information with ground-truth actions and improved action prediction, and pretraining VLAs with MVP-LAM latent actions improves downstream manipulation performance on the SIMPLER and LIBERO-Long benchmarks.
